title: microstachys prostrata
intro: Microstachys prostrata, also known as the creeping microstachys or creeping calycophyllum, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to Central and South America, known for its small, green leaves and white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help to alleviate various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Microstachys prostrata can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
